notes: No ADR needed. The clean command is a recovery utility consistent with existing contracts: ADR-001 already owns the LOCK file contract (root-level, managed by entrypoint); ADR-004 already defines the recovery path semantics (git reset + checkout main). Key implementation notes: (1) clean_run must execute BEFORE workspace_init in main() — the cli.bats tests for --clean must use a minimal git-only fixture, not the full workspace setup, to avoid workspace validation failures; (2) clippy.sh must call git_is_repo before dispatching to clean_run, so --clean on a non-git directory should exit 1 with an ERROR message; (3) the --force flag is the only gate for clean_discard_changes — without it, the function warns and skips destructive reset, which is the correct safe default; (4) clean_delete_branch is intentionally separate from clean_run and requires the caller to supply the branch name — this is correct since the branch name is only known after prd.json is read, which happens after workspace_init; do not attempt to auto-detect and delete the branch from inside clean_run.
